summary: Iteration 4 (custom Brevo-based OTP auth) FULL E2E PASS. Backend 22/22 pytest: 14 new OTP-flow tests + 8 regression. Brevo emails actually dispatching (signup returns 200; if Brevo key were invalid we'd see 502 from _send_otp_email). DB-injected happy-path verify confirmed: bcrypt-hashed codes (starts with $2), 10-min expires_at, attempts:0, last_sent_at present. Wrong code returns 401 'Incorrect code. N attempts remaining.' and increments DB attempts. Resend within 60s returns 429 'Please wait Ns before requesting another code.' Duplicate-unverified signup returns 200 (resends OTP); duplicate-verified returns 409. Forgot-password silently 200 for both registered and unregistered emails. Reset-password with correct code rotates Supabase password (new pw works, old fails) and deletes OTP record. Frontend verified at https://replay-quiz-1.preview.emergentagent.com: signup redirects to /verify-email with email pre-shown; OTP boxes verify-otp-0..5 accept input and auto-advance; resend countdown starts at 60s and ticks down (verified 60→57 in 2.5s); wrong code shows error 'Incorrect code. 3 attempts remaining.'; back link returns to /auth; /forgot-password → /reset-password navigation works; all reset-password testids present (reset-otp-0..5, reset-new-password-input, reset-confirm-password-input, reset-finalize-button, reset-email-input); pre-confirmed sign-in regression (creator@rocketsense.test) lands on /dashboard with quizzes rendered. No frontend or backend bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 669 lines — still below the 700-line threshold but approaching it. Consider moving the OTP auth section (lines 153-501) into a dedicated auth_router.py module for maintainability.

OTP cooldown check in /auth/resend-code uses last_sent_at from the existing record, but resend for 'recovery' type returns ok silently when user does not exist — which is good for non-enumeration; however the cooldown is checked BEFORE the existence check, meaning an attacker can still distinguish a 429 cooldown response (email exists in DB) from a 200 silent response (email does not exist). Minor enumeration leak via timing/status differentiation. Recommend always returning {ok:true} regardless when there's no real OTP record yet.

/auth/verify-code success path for type=signup calls Supabase admin PUT email_confirm=true. If Supabase returns >=300 we surface 502 but the OTP record has already been verified (still in DB). Consider deleting the OTP record after successful Supabase confirm (currently done — line 408 OK), but if Supabase fails we DO NOT increment attempts so a retry just hits the same error. Safe enough but worth noting.

bcrypt with default 12 rounds on each OTP store call is ~250ms CPU — acceptable for an SMTP-bound op but could be reduced to 10 rounds for OTPs since the code is throwaway and short-lived. (Not a security risk; just perf.)
_supa_admin opens a new httpx.AsyncClient per call — fine for low volume; consider a module-level shared client if traffic ramps.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (signup→verify, OTP auto-advance, countdown, wrong-code error, back link, forgot→reset navigation, all reset testids, sign-in regression — all verified)

test_credentials: creator@rocketsense.test / RocketTest123! still works (sign-in landed on /dashboard). Ephemeral signup emails used effectifyui+rliqtest<ts>@gmail.com (deleted via Supabase admin API in test_zz_cleanup_test_user).
seed_data_creation: Test created and self-cleaned one Supabase user (effectifyui+rliqtest<ts>@gmail.com) plus associated verification_codes docs. Frontend test created a second user (effectifyui+rliqfe<ts>@gmail.com) which remains in Supabase as an UNVERIFIED account — main agent may want to add periodic cleanup of unverified accounts older than ~24h, or manually delete this test user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Custom OTP auth (Brevo email + Supabase admin API) is fully working E2E. To exercise the happy-path verify without reading a real email, use the pattern in /app/backend/tests/test_otp_auth.py::_inject_code which upserts a bcrypt-hashed known code directly into MongoDB verification_codes (email,type) and then POSTs /api/auth/verify-code with the plaintext. Cooldown of 60s on resend means tests that hit resend twice need to inject last_sent_at backward, or use a different email. Brevo sender is effectifyui@gmail.com — use plus-addressing (effectifyui+anything@gmail.com) to avoid filling the real inbox.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. One initial frontend test reported reset-otp-0 missing — re-verification with a 2.5s wait confirmed all 6 boxes render reliably; root cause was a navigation-timing race in the initial check (queried immediately after wait_for_url before React mounted OtpInput children). No code change needed.
